method_atom: an analysis of the synchronization transition between two coupled identical chaotic dynamical systems, in which a mean-field (annealed) approximation reduces the coupled dynamics to a low-dimensional order parameter and predicts that the synchronized state emerges through a transcritical bifurcation as coupling is varied, complemented by a finite-size scaling study of deviations from the mean-field prediction and a perturbative treatment of weak quenched disorder.
object_atom: a pair of coupled identical Kauffman networks (random Boolean networks) driven into their chaotic phase, including partially disordered variants.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the mean-field/annealed reduction and transcritical-bifurcation route to synchronization of coupled chaotic systems plus finite-size analysis; the domain-specific object is the coupled random Boolean (Kauffman) networks the analysis is applied to.
